summary: PR 2443 primarily decompiled and matched more of the Home-Run Contest ground/stage unit `main/melee/gr/grhomerun`. The PR body was empty and there were no human review comments, but the diff shows new C implementations for major stage setup/update/object-factory/collision-callback functions plus header/type fixes. The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code rising to 66.32% (+0.02%, +748 bytes), with 4 new full matches and large partial improvements for `grHomeRun_8021CB20`, `grHomeRun_8021D680`, and `grHomeRun_8021E500`.

key_files.role: Main work: added Home-Run stage includes/data, a `grHomeRun_MainGroundVars` overlay, implemented `grHomeRun_8021CB20`, `grHomeRun_8021D680`, `grHomeRun_8021E500`, and `fn_8021E994`, and made matching-sensitive tweaks to `grHomeRun_8021EA30`, `grHomeRun_8021EC58`, and `grHomeRun_8021EDD4`.
Replaced `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes with exact signatures for `grHomeRun_8021E500`, `fn_8021E994`, `grHomeRun_8021EC58`, and `grHomeRun_8021EDD4`; added `mp/forward.h`.
Extended `struct grHomeRun_GroundVars` with an `f32 xD0` field used by the new ground segment object positioning code.
Include-order cleanup around Popo special headers.
Formatting cleanup for `GXColor` compound literals in `fn_8018DF68`.
Formatting cleanup for function-pointer casts in `gmClassic_801B3500`.
Formatting cleanup for a function-pointer cast in `grIceMt_801FA364`.
Blank-line cleanup only in the shown hunk.
Blank-line cleanup only in the shown hunk.
Blank-line cleanup only in the shown hunk.
Blank-line cleanup only in the shown hunk.

what_changed: Replaced nonmatching markers for `grHomeRun_8021CB20`, `grHomeRun_8021D680`, `grHomeRun_8021E500`, and `fn_8021E994` with C implementations in `grhomerun.c`.

`grHomeRun_8021CB20` now sets up the main Home-Run ground object: allocates object arrays, scales JOBJs, installs `mpJointSetCb1`, creates a camera/text GObj, loads `SIS_GrHomerunData`, creates/repositions repeating foreground/rear stage objects, and initializes text/flag state.

`grHomeRun_8021D680` now updates Home-Run stage state each frame: pulls fighter/item positions, updates camera subjects, computes displayed distance through `grHomeRun_8021EA30`, creates the text object lazily, streams ground segment GObjs based on `Camera_GetTransformInterest`, moves rear background GObjs, and updates mpLib/joint bounds.

`grHomeRun_8021E500` now chooses a ground object id from `arg0`, `grHr_804D6ADC`, and a four-part pattern, creates the GObj through `grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline`, scales and positions its JObj, and stores `xC4`, `xC6`, and `xD0` in the Home-Run ground vars.

`fn_8021E994` is now typed as an mpLib callback and sets the main Home-Run vars flag `xE8_flags.b0` when the Home-Run bat/target GObj collision condition matches the shown `collKind`/ground enum checks.
`grHomeRun_8021C754` now calls `grHomeRun_8021EDD4()` with no argument, and the header prototype was updated accordingly.
`grHomeRun_8021EA30` was adjusted with explicit temporaries and multiplication order changes; the bot reported it moved from 99.20% to 100.00% matched.

`grHomeRun_8021EC58` changed from `u32 score` to `s32 arg0`, uses a static `grHr_804D49D0` `"%d"` format string, sets text `pos_z` to `0.0F`, and keeps repeated scale calculations in matching-sensitive form.
Added exact prototypes and forward declarations for `HSD_GObj* grHomeRun_8021E500(s16)` and `void fn_8021E994(Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32)`.
Several unrelated files only received include-order, cast-spacing, compound-literal spacing, or blank-line cleanup.
smart_moves: Introduced `grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline` under `#pragma dont_inline` so call sites can force a non-inlined call shape while still reusing `grHomeRun_8021C82C`.

Used a local `grHomeRun_MainGroundVars` overlay for the main stage object instead of over-renaming every global ground union field; the diff only added the concrete `xD0` field needed by segment objects in `gr/types.h`.
Matched callback plumbing by giving `fn_8021E994` an exact mpLib-style signature and adding `mp/forward.h` rather than leaving it as `UNK_PARAMS`.

Kept matching-sensitive oddities visible, including line-numbered `HSD_ASSERT` calls, explicit casts, repeated `Ground_801C0498()` calls, and the no-op `arg2->x34_flags = arg2->x34_flags` in the null-ground branch.
Modeled Home-Run ground streaming with fixed-size allocations matching the evidence (`HSD_MemAlloc(0x100)` for 64 GObj slots and `HSD_MemAlloc(0x40)` for 16 JObj GObj slots).
Moved the text format string to a static `grHr_804D49D0[] = "%d"`, helping `.sdata` placement instead of embedding the literal directly in the call.
Split float expressions into temporaries (`new_var`, `temp_f31`, `temp_f31_2`) where the diff shows algebraically equivalent code was not enough for matching.
Preserved stage-specific constants such as `2400.0F`, `160.0F`, `2150.99F`, `0.85F`, `0.304788`, `30.0F`, and `1.4F` directly in the new C, making future comparison against assembly easier.
decomp_lessons: For stage ground code, separate the main stage-object vars from per-segment ground vars when the same union member is reused at different callback indices; a local overlay can be useful while names remain uncertain.

Do not collapse repeated scale/time calls or simplify float algebra too aggressively in matching work; the `grHomeRun_8021EA30` and `grHomeRun_8021EC58` changes show expression shape can decide a final few bytes.
`#pragma dont_inline` wrapper functions are acceptable tactical tools when a real helper must remain callable but certain call sites need a non-inlined code shape.
When replacing `UNK_PARAMS`, include the exact forward header needed by the new signature; here `mp/forward.h` enabled the `mpLib_GroundEnum` callback prototype.
Line-numbered `HSD_ASSERT` macros can be part of matching evidence; keep their constants aligned with the target instead of treating them as generic safety checks.
Static data placement matters: pulling `"%d"` into `grHr_804D49D0` and declaring `grHr_804DBC74/78/7C/80/84` as externs contributed to `.sdata`/`.sdata2` improvement per the bot report.
Header signature changes should be propagated with call-site cleanup, as shown by converting `grHomeRun_8021EDD4(int)` to `grHomeRun_8021EDD4(void)` and updating `grHomeRun_8021C754`.
naming_conventions: `grHomeRun_8021....` address-based names were retained for not-yet-semantic Home-Run functions.
`fn_8021E994` retained the generic `fn_` prefix even after typing, indicating the symbol still lacked a stronger semantic name in this PR.
`grHr_804D....` is used for Home-Run stage globals and static data such as `grHr_804D49D0` and `grHr_804D6AE4`.
`grHomeRun_MainGroundVars` names a local stage-specific overlay while uncertain fields keep offset-style names like `xCC`, `xD0`, and `xE8_flags`.
Bitfields in the local overlay use `b0` through `b7`, matching the project habit of preserving bit positions when semantics are still unknown.
The helper suffix `_noinline` explicitly documents a codegen/matching wrapper rather than a gameplay abstraction.
Headers keep address comments such as `/* 21E500 */` beside declarations.
Style cleanups favor spaced function-pointer casts like `u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8)` and spaced compound literals like `(GXColor) { 255, 255, 0, 255 }`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, `#pragma dont_inline reset`, and `#pragma pop` around `grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline`.
Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)` in `grHomeRun_8021CB20`, consistent with stack-shape matching tactics used elsewhere in the project.
Preserved explicit `HSD_ASSERT` line constants including `0x17A`, `0x17B`, `0x1A2`, `0x1AB`, `0x239`, and `0x3D5`-`0x3D7`.
Used explicit cast/order forms such as `(s32) (f32) arg0`, `(f32) i < ...`, `(mpLib_Callback) fn_8021E994`, and `*(u8*) &gp->x10_flags |= 4`.
Split the distance normalization in `grHomeRun_8021EA30` into `new_var = 160.0F * time; new_var = ((f32) grHr_804D6ADC) * new_var; result /= grHr_804D6AE4 * new_var;`.
Kept repeated `Ground_801C0498()` calls instead of caching one scale value across all text/camera/background calculations.
Declared `static HSD_CameraDescPerspective cobj_desc` inside `grHomeRun_8021CB20` to match static camera descriptor data.
Used `HSD_MemAlloc(0x100)` and `HSD_MemAlloc(0x40)` literal sizes rather than count-based `sizeof` expressions.
Retained the unusual self-assignment `arg2->x34_flags = arg2->x34_flags` in `fn_8021E994`, likely because it affected emitted code in the matched callback.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report: matched code became 66.32% (+0.02%, +748 bytes). New 100% matches were `fn_8021E994` (+156), `grHomeRun_8021C754` (+6), `grHomeRun_8021EA30` (+2), and `grHomeRun_8021EDD4` (+1).

Unmatched improvements were `grHomeRun_8021CB20` to 88.42% (+2567), `grHomeRun_8021D680` to 90.03% (+1887), `grHomeRun_8021E500` to 93.52% (+1096), `.sdata2` to 89.66% (+50), and `.sdata` to 54.10% (+3).
No human review comments or inline review feedback were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: grHomeRun_8021E500 grHr_804D6ADC gobj_id xC4 xC6 xD0
